summary: trap-rollback-inventory now excludes operational shell scripts that explicitly declare read_only=true and mutates_state=false, so --apply help text alone no longer makes a read-only validator look like a rollback gap.
why_flywheel_centric: The trap adoption parent is an ecosystem steering metric. Its classifier must separate real state mutation from canonical CLI contract text, otherwise Flywheel routes work toward false gaps.

proof.summary: 11/0 assertions including a fixture where a tracked operational shell script mentions --apply but declares read_only=true and mutates_state=false, and is excluded from mutating-like counts.
13/0 assertions; the validator still emits canonical CLI envelopes after adding mutates_state=false to its info contract.
9/0 assertions; trap-rollback-inventory remains wired as a receiver.
Syntax and ShellCheck are clean for the edited surfaces.
Live inventory now reports with_trap=98, without_trap=282, mutating_like_scripts=380, declared_read_only_excluded_count=2, coverage_pct=25.79.
No whitespace errors and no active dependency cycles.
